children.heading: Enforcement under Social Security Act
children.label: 2 Enforcement under Social Security Act
children.text: Any violation by a covered entity under thus subchapter is subject to enforcement and penalties under section 1176 and 1177 of the Social Security Act [, 1320d–6].1242 U.S.C. 1320d–5So in original. Probably should be “this”.1So in original. Probably should be “sections”.2
The amendments made by subsection (a) shall apply to penalties imposed on or after the date that is 24 months after .February 17, 2009
Not later than 18 months after , the Secretary of Health and Human Services shall promulgate regulations to implement such amendments.February 17, 2009

children.heading: Distribution of certain civil monetary penalties collected
children.label: c Distribution of certain civil monetary penalties collected

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to the regulation promulgated pursuant to paragraph (3), any civil monetary penalty or monetary settlement collected with respect to an offense punishable under this subchapter or section 1176 of the Social Security Act () insofar as such section relates to privacy or security shall be transferred to the Office for Civil Rights of the Department of Health and Human Services to be used for purposes of enforcing the provisions of this subchapter and subparts C and E of part 164 of title 45, Code of Federal Regulations, as such provisions are in effect as of .42 U.S.C. 1320d–5February 17, 2009

children.heading: GAO report
children.label: 2 GAO report
children.text: Not later than 18 months after , the Comptroller General shall submit to the Secretary a report including recommendations for a methodology under which an individual who is harmed by an act that constitutes an offense referred to in paragraph (1) may receive a percentage of any civil monetary penalty or monetary settlement collected with respect to such offense.February 17, 2009

children.heading: Establishment of methodology to distribute percentage of CMPS collected to harmed individuals
children.label: 3 Establishment of methodology to distribute percentage of CMPS collected to harmed individuals
children.text: Not later than 3 years after , the Secretary shall establish by regulation and based on the recommendations submitted under paragraph (2), a methodology under which an individual who is harmed by an act that constitutes an offense referred to in paragraph (1) may receive a percentage of any civil monetary penalty or monetary settlement collected with respect to such offense.February 17, 2009

children.heading: Application of methodology
children.label: 4 Application of methodology
children.text: The methodology under paragraph (3) shall be applied with respect to civil monetary penalties or monetary settlements imposed on or after the effective date of the regulation.

children.heading: Tiered increase in amount of civil monetary penalties
children.label: d Tiered increase in amount of civil monetary penalties

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: 4 Effective date
children.text: The amendments made by this subsection shall apply to violations occurring after .February 17, 2009

children.heading: Enforcement through State attorneys general
children.label: e Enforcement through State attorneys general

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: 3 Effective date
children.text: The amendments made by this subsection shall apply to violations occurring after .February 17, 2009
